name: Databend Cloud SQL Analytics API
what_it_does: Open-source cloud data warehouse written in Rust with REST API for SQL analytics. Snowflake-compatible SQL dialect with serverless architecture, object storage backend, and competitive pricing.
use_cases: Running complex SQL analytics on large datasets via REST API without infrastructure management
Querying time-series and event data for BI agents and reporting automation
Loading and transforming data from object storage (S3, GCS, Azure Blob) via SQL
Building data pipelines with Databend's transformation capabilities
Cost-effective Snowflake alternative for analytics workloads
not_for: OLTP/transactional workloads requiring low-latency single-row operations
Organizations heavily invested in Snowflake ecosystem without migration appetite
Operational databases needing strong consistency and ACID for writes
best_when: An agent needs cloud data warehouse capabilities at lower cost than Snowflake, with Snowflake-compatible SQL and object storage backends.
avoid_when: You need strong Snowflake ecosystem integrations or dbt compatibility — Databend's ecosystem is still developing.

auth.notes: Username/password or API token for Cloud. Self-hosted uses same mechanism. HTTP REST API or native protocol clients. Databend Cloud adds token-based access per compute warehouse.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Community Edition: self-hosted, completely free; Cloud free trial available
pricing.paid_tiers: Cloud: ~$2/credit hour; storage costs at S3/GCS rates
pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 open-source for self-hosted. Cloud pricing competitive vs Snowflake. Pay only for compute when queries run (serverless). Storage on your own object storage.

performance.rate_limits: Concurrent queries limited by warehouse size; configurable

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API and HTTP client documented. Snowflake-compatible SQL means existing SQL knowledge transfers. Error responses are JSON with error codes. Query execution is synchronous for small results, async for large.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: SELECT queries are idempotent. INSERT with stage loading can be made idempotent with deduplication. DDL operations are idempotent with IF NOT EXISTS.
known_agent_gotchas: Query results pagination for large result sets requires explicit handling
Snowflake compatibility is high but not 100% — some Snowflake-specific features differ
Self-hosted requires careful object storage configuration — S3/GCS/Azure Blob
Rust-based engine: faster cold start than Java-based warehouses but smaller community

security_score_components.security_notes: Apache 2.0 open-source in Rust — memory-safe codebase. SOC2 for Cloud edition. TLS enforced. Self-hosted provides full data control. Audit logging available. Column-level security in development.
